steps.title: Find the shared framing claim
steps.content: Both texts describe Impressionist painters making deliberate frame choices. Text 1 says many chose colorful frames to distinguish themselves from Salon conventions. Text 2 says frame colors were chosen for aesthetic reasons, to harmonize with the paintings.

steps.title: Reject unsupported agreement claims
steps.content: Choice A claims buyers especially wanted gold frames, which neither text says. Choice B reverses Text 2's relationship; frames were selected to suit paintings, not the reverse. Choice D says Moreau differed from most Impressionists, but Text 1 presents him as typical. The answer is C.
